perturbation.original_text: 1. Solicit within the State of Nevada, receive and transmit immediately and directly to MICOA, proposals for health care liability insurance contracts for which a commission is specified in the schedule of commissions provided by Exhibit A, attached and as amended or supplemented by such attachments from time to time.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. Solicit within the State of Nevada, receive and transmit immediately and directly to MICOA, proposals for health care liability insurance contracts, provided that only proposals adhering strictly to guidelines subsequently issued by MICOA will be considered. The commission for accepted proposals will be specified in the schedule of commissions provided by Exhibit A, attached and as amended or supplemented by such attachments from time to time.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the Agency is authorized to solicit proposals, but the changed text adds a condition that proposals must adhere strictly to guidelines subsequently issued by MICOA to be considered. This creates a contradiction because it's unclear whether the Agency is authorized to solicit all proposals, or only those that meet unspecified future guidelines. This pertubation adds a condition, creating uncertantity, as the original text does not contain this condition. In section A, it states the Agency is authorized to solicit proposals for health care liability insurance contracts, the added condition restricts this authority, which contradicts the original authorization.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A. AUTHORITY OF AGENCY Subject to requirements imposed by law, the underwriting rules, procedures and regulations of MICOA and this agreement, the Agency is authorized to: 1. Solicit within the State of Nevada, receive and transmit immediately and directly to MICOA, proposals for health care liability insurance contracts, provided that only proposals adhering strictly to guidelines subsequently issued by MICOA will be considered. The commission for accepted proposals will be specified in the schedule of commissions provided by Exhibit A, attached and as amended or supplemented by such attachments from time to time. 2. Produce and deliver certificates of insurance and written binders in accordance with MICOA underwriting requirements. The Agency is not authorized to accept or bind any risk or to otherwise obligate MICOA without specific authority from MICOA. 3. Provide all usual and customary services of an Agency on all policies placed with MICOA subject to the following: a. MICOA will not be responsible for Agency expenses including but not limited to rent, transportation, employee hire or solicitor's fees, postage, telegrams, telephone, advertising, licensing fees or any other Agency expenses whatsoever. b. The Agency will not undertake or initiate advertising of any nature in connection with business or policies related to MICOA without the approval of MICOA. 4. To promptly report all claims and losses of which the Agency has knowledge and properly notify MICOA when the Agency receives notice of the commencement of any related legal action. Agency shall refrain from admitting or denying liability on the part of the company in connection with any claim or lawsuit. 5. In return for the exclusive appointment of Agency by MICOA to sell its professional liability products listed on the &sbsp;attached Commission Schedule in Nevada, Agency agrees not to sell any competing professional liability products in Nevada, without the written consent of MICOA. Provided that, if a particular risk has been submitted to MICOA and MICOA has declined that risk, then Agency may search appropriate markets for placement of that risk, and may place that risk with another insurance company. 6. Designated Agent representatives upon request from MICOA will be expected to participate in MICOA's Nevada Market Managers Group activities and to attend all scheduled meetings. 7. MICOA will share on a project basis development costs of all promotional materials and some advertising costs related to Nevada sales, provided that all such expenditures or budgets for them are approved by MICOA in writing in advance. 8. Agency may solicit subagencies for appointment, subject to MICOA's prior written approval of each subagency following disclosure to and review by MICOA of information requested by MICOA for each proposed subagency. All such appointments by Agent shall stipulate that MICOA may terminate the subagency at any time without cause upon at least 90 days notice and that the subagency shall comply with all MICOA requirements and duties owed MICOA by Agency concerning solicitation, communications, and service to insureds. Subagencies shall also be required to submit all proposals immediately and directly to MICOA.
perturbation.justification: The original text authorized the Agency to solicit proposals for health care liability insurance contracts. The changed text adds a condition that only proposals adhering strictly to guidelines subsequently issued by MICOA will be considered. This condition restricts the Agency's original authorization to solicit proposals, creating a direct contradiction as the original authorization did not contain this condition. The contradicted text, Section A, initially grants the Agency the authority to solicit proposals without any prior constraints, but the changed text now limits this authority based on future, unspecified guidelines from MICOA. This presents a clear and direct conflict, making the original authorization conditional and uncertain.

perturbation.original_text: b. Immediately if either party gives detailed written notice to the other of alleged gross and willful misconduct, fraud or material misrepresentation.
perturbation.changed_text: b. Immediately if either party gives detailed written notice to the other of alleged gross and willful misconduct, fraud or material misrepresentation, provided however that the receiving party has 30 days to cure said misconduct, fraud or material misrepresentation.
perturbation.explanation: The original text allows for immediate termination upon notice of misconduct, fraud, or misrepresentation. The modified text adds a 30-day cure period, creating a contradiction. It's unclear whether termination is immediate or subject to a cure period, thus introducing ambiguity. If misconduct, fraud or material misrepresentation take place, then by inserting this condition, it allows to solve the conflict, which contradicts the original text on point F regarding termination. The contradition being made here is on the immediate termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: F. TERMINATION OF AGREEMENT 1. This agreement shall terminate: a. Automatically if any public authority cancels or declines to renew the Agency's license or Certificate of Authority. b. Immediately if either party gives detailed written notice to the other of alleged gross and willful misconduct, fraud or material misrepresentation, provided however that the receiving party has 30 days to cure said misconduct, fraud or material misrepresentation. 2. This Agreement shall terminate, subject to any automatic renewal or extension for one year as required by law, upon either party giving at least one hundred twenty (120) days advance written notice to the other, if not otherwise contrary to applicable law or this Agreement. 3. If the Agency is delinquent in either accounting or payment of monies due MICOA, MICOA may by written notice to the Agency immediately terminate, suspend or modify any of the provisions of this agreement. Such action shall not be taken by MICOA over minor differences between the records of the Agency and MICOA. 4. All supplies, including forms and policies furnished by MICOA and any copies or other reproductions of them, shall remain the property of MICOA and shall be returned to MICOA or its representative upon demand.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed for immediate termination upon notice of misconduct, fraud, or misrepresentation. The changed text adds a 30-day cure period, creating a contradiction because point 'b' of section F.1 in the contradicted text states termination happens 'Immediately' but the changed text imposes a 30-day cure period, which contradicts the immediate nature of termination.

perturbation.original_text: The Agency will maintain valid errors and omissions insurance, with minimum limits of $1,000,000 per incident, and a fidelity and electronic crime policy through an insurer, both of which shall contain terms and limits of coverage acceptable to MICOA covering the Agency's solicitors and each of its employees.
perturbation.changed_text: The Agency will maintain valid errors and omissions insurance with limits to be decided solely by the Agency. The Agency will also secure a fidelity and electronic crime policy, if deemed necessary by the Agency, through an insurer, both of which shall contain terms and limits of coverage.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies that the agency must maintain errors and omissions insurance with minimum limits of $1,000,000 per incident and requires a fidelity and electronic crime policy. The modified version removes the specified minimum limits for errors and omissions insurance and makes the fidelity and electronic crime policy optional. This contradiction introduces ambiguity regarding the required insurance coverage. Section E, which originally stipulates certain required policies, now contradicts itself by stating that the policies are up to the Agency to decide. By having conflicting information as to the type of policies being required, create an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: E. AGENCY'S ERRORS AND OMISSION, AND FIDELITY & ELECTRONIC CRIME INSURANCE The Agency will maintain valid errors and omissions insurance with limits to be decided solely by the Agency. The Agency will also secure a fidelity and electronic crime policy, if deemed necessary by the Agency, through an insurer, both of which shall contain terms and limits of coverage. The Agency shall provide MICOA a copy of each policy; doing so on a regular and current basis shall be a precondition to all of Agency's rights under this Agreement, including but not limited to the payment of all earned commissions.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated specific insurance requirements (errors and omissions insurance with $1,000,000 minimum limits, and a fidelity and electronic crime policy). The changed text removes the specified minimum limits and makes the fidelity and electronic crime policy optional, contradicting the original mandatory requirements. Section E, the contradicted text, mirrors the changed text, thus it establishes an in-text contradiction because the agency's obligations regarding insurance are rendered ambiguous, creating uncertainty about the required coverage. This is a clear ambiguity, as the contract now contradicts itself by stating that policies are 'to be decided solely by the Agency,' despite previously implying a mandatory structure. This falls under Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction definition.
